fields.name: Unique Key
fields.label: Unique Key
fields.description: A unique integer identifier assigned to each complaint record.

fields.name: Created Date
fields.label: Created Timestamp
fields.description: The date and time when the complaint was created in the system.

fields.name: Closed Date
fields.label: Closed Timestamp
fields.description: The date and time when the complaint was closed or resolved.

fields.name: Agency
fields.label: Agency Code
fields.description: Abbreviated code for the agency responsible for handling the complaint (e.g., NYPD, HPD).

fields.name: Agency Name
fields.label: Agency Full Name
fields.description: Full name of the agency that handled the complaint.
fields.examples:
New York City Police Depa… [265038]
Department of Housing Pre… [258019]
Department of Transportat… [132462]
Other… [103974]
Department of Environment… [75895]

fields.name: Complaint Type
fields.label: Primary Complaint Category
fields.description: Primary category of the complaint (e.g., Noise - Residential, HEAT/HOT WATER).
fields.examples:
Other… [563561]
Noise - Residential [89439]
HEAT/HOT WATER [56639]
Illegal Parking [45032]
Blocked Driveway [42356]

fields.name: Descriptor
fields.label: Secondary Complaint Descriptor
fields.description: More detailed descriptor providing additional context about the issue.
fields.examples:
Other… [671870]
Loud Music/Party [93646]
ENTIRE BUILDING [36885]
HEAT [35088]
No Access [31631]

fields.name: Location Type
fields.label: Location Type Category
fields.description: Type of location where the incident occurred (e.g., RESIDENTIAL BUILDING, Street/Sidewalk).
fields.examples:
RESIDENTIAL BUILDING [255562]
(NULL)… [239131]
Street/Sidewalk [145653]
Residential Building/Hous… [92765]
Street [92190]

fields.name: Incident Zip
fields.label: Incident ZIP Code
fields.description: 5‑digit ZIP code for the incident location; part of the address components.

fields.name: Incident Address
fields.label: Incident Street Address
fields.description: Full street address of the incident location, including number and street name. Combine with City, Borough and ZIP Code to form a complete address.
fields.examples:
Other… [819046]
(NULL)… [174700]
655 EAST  230 STREET [1538]
78-15 PARSONS BOULEVARD [694]
672 EAST  231 STREET [663]

fields.name: Street Name
fields.label: Street Name at Incident
fields.description: Name component of the street involved in the incident; part of the address. Use together with other address fields for the full location.
fields.examples:
Other… [784684]
(NULL)… [174720]
BROADWAY [9702]
GRAND CONCOURSE [5851]
OCEAN AVENUE [3946]

fields.name: Cross Street 1
fields.label: Cross Street 1 (Intersection)
fields.description: Primary cross street at an intersection where the incident occurred; used along with Cross Street 2, Intersection Street 1/2 to describe intersection geometry.

fields.name: Cross Street 2
fields.label: Cross Street 2 (Intersection)
fields.description: Secondary cross street at an intersection where the incident occurred; used along with Cross Street 1, Intersection Street 1/2 to describe intersection geometry.

fields.name: Intersection Street 1
fields.label: Intersection Street 1
fields.description: First street name at a reported intersection. Combined with the second intersection street and cross streets for full intersection context.

fields.name: Intersection Street 2
fields.label: Intersection Street 2
fields.description: Second street name at a reported intersection. Combined with the first intersection street, cross streets, and address components for complete location detail.

fields.name: Address Type
fields.label: Address Classification
fields.description: Classification of the address type (e.g., ADDRESS, INTERSECTION).

fields.name: City
fields.label: City/Borough of Incident
fields.description: City or borough where the incident took place; part of the full address.

fields.name: Landmark
fields.label: Nearby Landmark
fields.description: Notable landmark near the incident location; may aid in identifying the site.
fields.examples:
(NULL)… [912779]
Other… [80165]
EAST  230 STREET [1545]
EAST  231 STREET [1291]
BROADWAY [1148]

fields.name: Facility Type
fields.label: Facility Type Category
fields.description: Type of facility involved in the complaint (e.g., DSNY Garage, School District).

fields.name: Status
fields.label: Complaint Status
fields.description: Current status of the complaint (e.g., Closed, Pending).
fields.enumeration:
Assigned
Closed
Closed - Testing
Email Sent
In Progress
Open
Pending
Started
Unassigned
Unspecified
fields.examples:
Closed [952522]
Pending [20119]
Open [12340]
In Progress [7841]
Assigned [6651]

fields.name: Due Date
fields.label: Due Timestamp
fields.description: Date and time by which a resolution was expected.

fields.name: Resolution Description
fields.label: Resolution Summary Text
fields.description: Text description summarizing how the complaint was resolved.
fields.min: A DOB violation was issued for failing to comply with an existing Stop Work Order.
fields.max: Your request was submitted to the Department of Homeless Services. The City?s outreach team will assess the homeless individual and offer appropriate assistance within 2 hours. If you asked to know the outcome of your request, you will get a call within 2 hours. No further status will be available through the NYC 311 App, 311, or 311 Online.
fields.examples:

The Police Department res… [91408]
The Department of Housing… [72962]
The Police Department res… [63868]
Service Request status fo… [52155]

fields.name: Resolution Action Updated Date
fields.label: Last Resolution Update Time
fields.description: Timestamp of the most recent update to the resolution action.

fields.name: Community Board
fields.label: Community Board Designation
fields.description: Community board designation associated with the incident location.

fields.name: BBL
fields.label: Borough Block Lot Identifier
fields.description: Borough, Block and Lot number identifying the parcel where the incident occurred.

fields.name: Borough
fields.label: Incident Borough
fields.description: NYC borough in which the incident took place; used along with BBL for parcel identification.
fields.enumeration:
BRONX
BROOKLYN
MANHATTAN
QUEENS
STATEN ISLAND
Unspecified

fields.label: X Coordinate (State Plane)

fields.name: Y Coordinate (State Plane)
fields.label: Y Coordinate (State Plane)
fields.description: Y coordinate of the incident in State Plane projection.

fields.label: Open Data Submission Channel

Description.response:
**Description**

The dataset contains one million NYC 311 complaint records collected between January 2010 and December 2020.  
Each record is uniquely identified by an integer key and includes timestamps for when the complaint was created (and, where available, closed), the agency responsible for handling it, a primary complaint category with an optional descriptor, and detailed geographic information such as address components, borough, ZIP code, latitude/longitude and State‑Plane coordinates.  The majority of complaints are filed by NYPD, HPD or DOT, while many other agencies appear only sparsely; most records fall into a handful of high‑frequency complaint types, with the remainder labeled “Other”.  Several fields—particularly address components, due dates, resolution text, and community board identifiers—contain substantial missing or generic values.

---

### Notable Characteristics

- **Temporal spread** – Created and closed timestamps span roughly eleven years (2010–2020) with a mean around late November 2015 and a standard deviation of about three years; skewness is near zero, indicating an approximately symmetric distribution.
- **Agency imbalance** – NYPD, HPD and DOT together account for more than 65 % of complaints; the remaining agencies are represented in a long tail that feeds into an “Other” bucket.
- **Complaint heterogeneity** – Over half of the records fall under “Other” in both the primary complaint type and descriptor fields, revealing many rare or unclassified issues.
- **Geographic sparsity** – Incident ZIP codes are missing for ~5.5 % of records, incident addresses for ~17 %, city names for ~6 % and address types for ~12 %.  Latitude/longitude coordinates exist for ~75 % of cases; State‑Plane X/Y coordinates are present for ~85 %.
- **Submission channel skew** – Phone submissions dominate (~50 %) while online, mobile, unknown and other channels make up the rest.
- **Due date gaps** – Approximately two‑thirds of records lack a due date, limiting deadline‑based analyses.
- **Resolution metadata variability** – Resolution descriptions are highly diverse with thousands of unique strings; over 20 % of these fields are null.  The last resolution update timestamps also show high sparsity and many “Other” categories.
- **PII/PHI considerations** – Street addresses, precise coordinates, borough/block/lot identifiers, and community board designations can potentially identify specific locations or individuals when combined with other data sources; these fields should be treated as sensitive.
- **Outlier dates** – Some date columns contain placeholder extremes (1900‑01‑01 or 2100‑01‑01) to indicate missing values, which can bias statistical summaries if not properly handled.
- **Duplicate absence** – The unique key constraint guarantees no duplicate records; however, the high cardinality of categorical fields may introduce sparsity in downstream modeling tasks.

---

Description (3-4 sentences):

"The dataset contains one million 311 complaint records collected by New York City between January 2010 and December 2020. Each record is uniquely identified by an integer key and includes timestamps for when the complaint was created and, where available, closed; agency codes and full names of the responsible city department; a primary complaint category and optional descriptor; geographic information such as address components, borough, ZIP code, latitude/longitude and State Plane coordinates; and metadata about how the complaint was submitted and its current status. The majority of records are from the NYPD, HPD or DOT agencies, with many other departments represented only sparsely, and most complaints fall into a small set of high‑frequency categories while the remaining 56 % are labeled “Other”. While timestamps span roughly eleven years, several fields—especially address components, due dates, resolution text, and community board identifiers—contain substantial missing or generic values."

Notable Characteristics:

- Timestamp distribution: Created Date mean ~2015‑11‑10 with SD ≈3 years; Closed Date similar; skewness near zero indicating roughly symmetric spread across the decade.
- Agency representation is highly uneven; NYPD (≈26 %), HPD (≈26 %) and DOT (≈13 %) account for over 65 % of complaints, leaving a long tail of other agencies in the “Other” category.
- Complaint Type and Descriptor fields are dominated by an “Other” bucket that covers more than half of the records, revealing many rare or unclassified complaint categories.
- Geographic fields show substantial sparsity: Incident Zip (≈5.5 % null), Incident Address (~17 %), City (~6 %) and Address Type (~12 %). Latitude/Longitude are present for ~75 % of records; State Plane coordinates exist for about 85 %.
- Submission channel is heavily skewed toward PHONE (≈50 %); the “Due Date” field is missing in roughly two‑thirds of records.
- Resolution description and last update timestamps are highly heterogeneous, with thousands of unique strings and >20 % null values.
- The dataset contains potential PII: street addresses, precise coordinates, and borough/block/lot identifiers that could be combined with other sources to locate individuals or properties. These fields should be treated as sensitive in downstream analyses.
- Several date columns use placeholder extremes (1900‑01‑01 or 2100‑01‑01) for missing data; these outliers can bias statistical summaries if not handled appropriately.
- No duplicate records are present due to the unique key constraint, but high cardinality of categorical fields may lead to sparsity in machine‑learning models.
